title: Vivi Pushes Toward Yuba
description: On pages 8–10, this part focuses on vivi pushes toward yuba, keeping the scene centered on the conflict, choices, and stakes of the Alabasta arc.
scene: Vivi Pushes Toward Yuba. The journey through the desert continues, mixing exhaustion, danger, and Vivi’s memories of the country she is trying to save. This section presents the immediate scene beat before the dialogue continues.

dialogue.speaker: Nami
dialogue.text: I'm afraid the weather is the domain of gods.

dialogue.speaker: Vivi
dialogue.text: Even as a king, there's nothing I can do about it.

dialogue.speaker: Vivi
dialogue.text: But my dad said the king could do anything!

dialogue.speaker: Luffy
dialogue.text: The things I can do are still limited to things achieved through human means.

dialogue.speaker: King Cobra
dialogue.text: Your majesty! A man calling himself as this boy's father has just entered the palace!

dialogue.speaker: King Cobra
dialogue.text: Forgive the Inso- Lance my manner- less son has shown, your majesty!

dialogue.speaker: Nami
dialogue.text: The village boy's father, Toto

dialogue.speaker: King Cobra
dialogue.text: I accept full respond- Bility for his actions so please punish me as your majesty sees fit!

dialogue.speaker: Usopp
dialogue.text: You idiot, you idiot, you idiot! Lower your head this instant and apologize!

dialogue.speaker: Vivi
dialogue.text: Wow!

dialogue.speaker: Zoro
dialogue.text: You said your name is Toto, correct...?

dialogue.speaker: King Cobra
dialogue.text: Yes, your majesty!

dialogue.speaker: Zoro
dialogue.text: The state will provision all homeless villages affected by the recent draught.

dialogue.speaker: Zoro
dialogue.text: In the meanwhile, they are permitted to move here to the capital and think about what to do with their lives from now on.

dialogue.speaker: King Cobra
dialogue.text: Your majesty's benevolent is truly astounding! We are grateful from the bottom of our hearts!

dialogue.speaker: Toto
dialogue.text: You think that's suddenly gonna fix everything? You don't have a clue how the villagers feel!

dialogue.speaker: Toto
dialogue.text: Kouza, come back!

dialogue.speaker: Toto
dialogue.text: I said come back!

dialogue.speaker: Nami
dialogue.text: Toto...

dialogue.speaker: King Cobra
dialogue.text: Y-yes, your majesty?

dialogue.speaker: Zoro
dialogue.text: That's a good son you've raised there. He said "how the villagers feel" just now.

dialogue.speaker: Zoro
dialogue.text: That means he came all the way here to speak with me for their sake.

dialogue.speaker: Koza
dialogue.text: Crybaby!

dialogue.speaker: Sanji
dialogue.text: What did you call me, pipsqueak?

dialogue.speaker: King Cobra
dialogue.text: Your majesty.

dialogue.speaker: Zoro
dialogue.text: Yes, Igaram?

dialogue.speaker: Zoro
dialogue.text: I'm afraid I must inform you that we...

dialogue.speaker: Luffy
dialogue.text: Currently do not have enough funds to fully provi- sim all the villagers...

dialogue.speaker: Zoro
dialogue.text: YOU FOOL! Writing out the money from the royal family's personal spendings if you have to!

dialogue.speaker: Zoro
dialogue.text: smack!

dialogue.speaker: Vivi
dialogue.text: Oww!

dialogue.speaker: Sanji
dialogue.text: As you wish...

summary: This part develops vivi pushes toward yuba and pushes the Alabasta conflict closer to its next decisive turn.
